reasoning: The business quality is undeniable and the position has been a exceptional winner. But 10.9% in one semiconductor equipment stock at 58x P/E, with the stock outrunning analyst targets, is not a "nothing to see here" situation. Hold the position — the AI metrology secular tailwind is real and Q1 earnings (May 2026) is the decisive catalyst. If Q1 comes in strong and guidance is raised, consider trimming 3-4 shares to bring NVMI below 8%. If Q1 disappoints, exit half immediately. ENTRY/EXIT CONDITIONS: If Q1 2026 beats ($222M+ revenue, EPS at or above $2.13 non-GAAP): reduce ~3-4 shares (bring to ~8%). If Q1 misses guidance floor OR stock breaks below $400: reduce 50% of position. No new buying.
exitConditions: If Q1 2026 beats ($222M+ revenue, EPS at or above $2.13 non-GAAP): reduce ~3-4 shares (bring to ~8%)
If Q1 misses guidance floor OR stock breaks below $400: reduce 50% of position

bullCase: Nova is the best operator in the portfolio — 31% revenue growth, 29% EPS growth, raising guidance, $1B+ cash, zero debt. Metrion platform adoption by leading Memory/Logic manufacturers validates the TAM expansion thesis. WFE growing 9%, hyperscaler capex $600B+. Record everything in FY2025. At 10.9% of portfolio with a 116% gain, the position has earned its space.
bearCase: P/E at 58x is nearly double the sector average of 29x. The stock trades ABOVE the average analyst price target — not below it, which is unusual. Any Q1 earnings stumble (revenue below $222M guidance floor, margin compression) triggers severe multiple compression from these levels. At 10.9% of portfolio, a 30% drawdown in NVMI alone costs you 3.3% of your entire net worth. The semiconductor equipment cycle is real — WFE at record levels is late-cycle risk, not secular forever.
